name: gold coins
shortDescription: Gold coins are stamped metal trust made portable, useful wherever scales, debt, and suspicion meet. The gold gives the stack its first argument, but their real power is the speed with which they quiet a difficult price. Common coinage like this draws money-changers, toll keepers, lenders, and anyone counting debts. It is small enough to vanish into a sleeve, purse, or false-bottom box. Gold gives the piece a clear physical identity. Its margin improves when matched to the buyer rather than thrown into the first open offer.
flavorText: “Gold coins spend quickly, but the wiser profit is knowing when not to show them.”

name: Sunwake Harbor Company Share
shortDescription: Sunwake Harbor Company Share is a readable volume with value in its title, binding, subject, and the hands that kept it dry. The pages make it more than cargo: they can inform a buyer, flatter a collector, or turn old ink into leverage. Singular book like this draws scribes, collectors, clerks, tutors, and merchants who know the value of paper. It is small enough to vanish into a sleeve, purse, or false-bottom box. A base value this high invites proof, caution, and quieter voices.
flavorText: “A book is quiet only until the right reader finds the sentence they needed.”

Its value comes from danger made tradable: cleaned, packed, named, and offered before it can become trouble again. Common animal goods like this draws practical buyers, collectors, and traders who know how to price a story. It is easy enough to move, but still worth wrapping before the road shakes it loose. Monster part gives the piece a clear physical identity. Its low price makes it useful for bundle math, favors, and small adjustments at the end of a bargain.
flavorText: “If salamander seems easy to price, wait until two buyers want it for different reasons.”

Its best price comes from condition, timing, and a merchant willing to explain why this exact piece matters. Common stone like this draws practical buyers, collectors, and traders who know how to price a story. It is awkward enough to change how a pack is loaded. Stone gives the piece a clear physical identity. Its margin improves when matched to the buyer rather than thrown into the first open offer.
flavorText: “A careful seller lets stone worktable sit in the light just long enough for greed to become curiosity.”

name: spindle
shortDescription: Spindle is textile stock with value in fiber, color, weave, and the promise of patient hands turning it into something better. Keep it clean and dry; cloth remembers every stain more honestly than sellers do. Common textile like this draws tailors, dyers, guild buyers, and households with patient hands. It is small enough to vanish into a sleeve, purse, or false-bottom box. Cloth gives the piece a clear physical identity. Its low price makes it useful for bundle math, favors, and small adjustments at the end of a bargain.
flavorText: “Never sell spindle as a name alone. Sell the condition, the timing, and the reason it reached your stall.”

Its price comes from condition and confidence, but also from the buyer's private estimate of coming trouble. Rare weapon like this draws guards, hunters, caravan escorts, and nervous quartermasters. It is awkward enough to change how a pack is loaded. Steel gives the piece a clear physical identity. Its margin improves when matched to the buyer rather than thrown into the first open offer.
flavorText: “Bow changes hands often; ledger entry 1729 gives scarce bow of peircing its own road and rumor.”
